title: Master Evidence Ledger & Claims Registry v1.0
subtitle: Institutional Truth Architecture
current_claim_route: claims-ledger.json + facts-registry — no public claim pages
purpose: Every meaningful factual claim traceable to evidence.
core_philosophy: Every meaningful factual claim can be traced back to evidence.
governing_principle: Authority from evidence quality and transparent review — not institutional assertion alone.
long_term_vision: Visitors explore the chain of evidence behind every important claim.

evidence_strength_levels.title: Primary legal authority
evidence_strength_levels.description: Supreme Court opinions, statutes, constitutional text.

evidence_strength_levels.title: Official government publications
evidence_strength_levels.description: Government reports, public records, official datasets.

evidence_strength_levels.title: Peer-reviewed academic literature
evidence_strength_levels.description: Law review articles, academic journals, university publications.

evidence_strength_levels.title: Multiple reliable secondary sources
evidence_strength_levels.description: Educational organizations, major news, verified secondary references.

evidence_strength_levels.title: Educational synthesis
evidence_strength_levels.description: Context-dependent synthesis — methodology must be documented.

contradictory_evidence_policy: Explain the nature of the disagreement
Encourage readers to consult primary materials
future_ai_integration: Build educational summaries grounded in verified information

claims.claim_statement: Citizens United held that government may not restrict independent political spending by corporations and unions.
claims.plain_language: The Supreme Court ruled that independent political spending by corporations and unions is protected speech.
claims.expanded_explanation: In Citizens United v. FEC (2010), the Court held that the First Amendment prohibits government restriction of independent expenditures by corporations and associations.
claims.educational_context: Foundation for understanding corporate independent expenditure doctrine after 2010.
claims.learn_next: SpeechNow.org v. FEC and Super PAC development

claims.claim_statement: Montana Initiative 194 would prohibit artificial persons from contributing to campaigns, ballot measures, or political parties.
claims.plain_language: Montana voters may consider a ballot measure restricting corporate and organizational political contributions.
claims.expanded_explanation: Initiative 194 seeks to exclude artificial persons from contributing to campaigns, ballot measures, or political parties in Montana.
claims.educational_context: Example of state-level response to Citizens United through ballot initiative.
claims.learn_next: Compare with Hawaii Act 11 legislative approach

claims.claim_statement: Hawaii Act 11 restricts corporate election spending; effective July 1, 2027.
claims.plain_language: Hawaii passed legislation restricting certain corporate election spending, with a future effective date.
claims.expanded_explanation: Hawaii Act 11 addresses corporate influence in elections; legislative text confirmation pending full primary source review.
claims.educational_context: Illustrates legislative (not ballot) path to corporate spending restrictions.
claims.learn_next: Obtain Hawaii legislative text as Level A/B evidence
claims.note: Journalism source — legislative text not yet primary-confirmed

catalog_gaps: Only 3/500 claims — vast majority of platform sentences not CLAIM-linked
13 FACT-* records — not all migrated to CLAIM-* format
No public /claims/[id] pages — transparency panels not built
Audit trail defined — 0 change history records
Scheduled review workflow planned — no automation
Level C (academic) — 0 peer-reviewed sources in evidence registry
Contradictory evidence policy defined — no live conflict records
Trust framework has A–D levels — ledger adds Level E synthesis

recommended_next_build.title: Component Specifications with Props/States
recommended_next_build.note: Map claim panels, evidence strength badges, audit history viewers, and transparency widgets to COMP-* from Build #17.
